title: Infer hidden race regimes from internal lap aggregates
summary: Public pit-stop work explicitly uses track-status information and notes that SC/VSC/yellow periods make stops strategically cheaper.
rationale: Public pit-stop work explicitly uses track-status information and notes that SC/VSC/yellow periods make stops strategically cheaper. Since the competition data omits explicit race-control flags, a hidden-state layer inferred from observable field behavior could recover part of that missing signal.
implementation_hint: Build per-race-lap aggregates available at prediction time: median and dispersion of `LapTime_Delta`, share of wet tyres, contemporaneous `PitStop` count, and position churn. Use thresholds or a small HMM to label laps as green, neutralized, wet-transition, or restart, then interact those states with stop-pressure features.
expected_effect: Recovers cheap-stop windows and explains many otherwise strange long-lap/pit-cluster cases without external joins.
risk: State inference can become race-specific leakage if validation splits do not leave whole race weekends out; thresholds may also confuse weather shocks with neutralizations.
